subtasks.description:
Create src/widgets/Window.mag — a framed window widget for the Alto TUI framework.

First create the src/widgets/ directory if it does not exist.

File: src/widgets/Window.mag
Namespace: Alto, Import: Go

Class definition:
  Window subclass: Object
    instanceVars: title contentView contentController frame buffer dragState

Class constructor:
  classMethod: title: aString content: aView controller: aController frame: aRect [
    | win |
    win := self new.
    win initTitle: aString content: aView controller: aController frame: aRect.
    ^win
  ]

  classMethod: new [
    | instance |
    instance := super new.
    instance initDefaults.
    ^instance
  ]

  method: initDefaults [
    title := ''.
    contentView := nil.
    contentController := nil.
    frame := nil.
    buffer := nil.
    dragState := nil
  ]

  method: initTitle: aString content: aView controller: aController frame: aRect [
    title := aString.
    contentView := aView.
    contentController := aController.
    frame := aRect.
    buffer := CellBuffer width: aRect width height: aRect height.
    dragState := nil
  ]

Accessors:
  method: buffer [ ^buffer ]
  method: frame [ ^frame ]
  method: title [ ^title ]
  method: contentView [ ^contentView ]
  method: contentController [ ^contentController ]

Rendering — method: displayOn: aCellBuffer [
  This method draws the window into aCellBuffer (which is self's own buffer).
  All temp vars declared at top of method.

  Steps:
  1. Declare all temps at top: | borderRect w h titleLen closeX i ch |
  2. Get w := frame width. h := frame height.
  3. Draw box-drawing border using aCellBuffer drawBox: method.
     Create a borderRect: Rectangle x: 0 y: 0 width: w height: h.
     Call aCellBuffer drawBox: borderRect style: nil.
     (This uses chars 9484/9488/9492/9496/9472/9474 which drawBox: already implements.)
  4. Title bar — first row inside top border (y=0 is the border, title goes on the border row itself between corners):
     Actually, looking at the spec more carefully: 'first row inside top border' means y=1.
     Write title text left-aligned starting at x: 1, y: 1.
     Use aCellBuffer putChar:x:y:style: for each character of title.
     titleLen := title size min: (w - 5) to leave room for close box.
     Loop i from 1 to titleLen, get ch := (title at: i) value, call aCellBuffer putChar: ch x: i y: 1 style: nil.
     (Note: Maggie String at: is 1-based per the existing CellBuffer putString: which uses 1-based indexing.)
  5. Close box [X] at right side of title bar row (y=1):
     closeX := w - 4.
     aCellBuffer putChar: 91 x: closeX y: 1 style: nil.    (91 = '[')
     aCellBuffer putChar: 88 x: closeX + 1 y: 1 style: nil. (88 = 'X')
     aCellBuffer putChar: 93 x: closeX + 2 y: 1 style: nil. (93 = ']')
  6. Content area — delegate to contentView if not nil:
     contentView isNil ifTrue: [^self].
     (Do NOT use ifNil:/ifNotNil:. Use isNil ifTrue:.)
     contentView displayOn: aCellBuffer.
     (The content view is responsible for knowing its offset within the buffer.
      Alternatively, the window could set contentView bounds before calling displayOn:.)

  Important: draw method (inherited pattern from View or called by Display):
  method: draw [
    buffer isNil ifTrue: [^self].
    buffer clear.
    self displayOn: buffer
  ]

Event handling:
  method: handleEvent: evt [
    All temps at top: | evtType mx my closeX closeY titleBarY |
    
    1. Check if evt responds to mouse position. The event dispatching should check event type.
       For simplicity, try to dispatch to contentController first:
       contentController isNil ifTrue: [^self].
       
    2. Mouse click on [X] close box:
       Check if event is a mouse click at the close box position.
       closeX is (frame width - 4) to (frame width - 2), closeY is 1 (title bar row).
       If click is within close box bounds, send self close.
       
    3. Mouse drag on title bar (y = 1, not on close box):
       If mouse down on title bar row (y=1, x from 1 to closeX-1):
         Set dragState to a Dictionary with #startX/#startY of mouse and #frameX/#frameY of frame origin.
       If mouse drag and dragState notNil:
         Calculate delta, update frame x: and frame y:.
       If mouse up: dragState := nil.
       
    4. Otherwise delegate: contentController handleEvent: evt.
  ]

  method: close [
    "Send close — subclasses or the display can override behavior."
    ^self
  ]

CRITICAL SYNTAX RULES:
- namespace: 'Alto' and import: 'Go' at top of file
- Use instanceVars: keyword form (not bracket class body syntax)
- ALL temp var declarations at TOP of each method, never mid-block
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use isNil ifTrue: / notNil ifTrue: pattern
- Character codes as integers (91 not $[ etc.)
- No $x character literals
- Follow the exact patterns from View.mag, Controller.mag, CellBuffer.mag
- CellBuffer drawBox: already exists and handles box-drawing chars — USE IT
- Array mutation uses copyWith:/reject: (not add:/remove:)
- String indexing: at: is 1-based in Maggie (per CellBuffer putString: pattern)
- Use 'value' to get char code from a Character object (based on CellBuffer putString: using 'charCode' — but verify; if charCode doesn't work, try 'value')

Reference files for patterns:
- src/display/CellBuffer.mag (drawBox:, putChar:, putString: APIs)
- src/display/Rectangle.mag (x, y, width, height, containsPointX:y:)
- src/mvc/View.mag (class structure, instanceVars form, initialize pattern)
- src/mvc/Controller.mag (handleEvent: pattern)
- src/display/Display.mag (shows how Display calls window buffer/frame/handleEvent:)

Create test/window_test.mag — comprehensive tests for Window widget.

File: test/window_test.mag

Follow the test pattern from test/mvc_phase3_test.mag exactly: simple test runner class T with assert:message: and assertEqual:to:message:, plus a runner class.

Test categories:

1. CONSTRUCTION TESTS:
   - Window title:content:controller:frame: creates a non-nil instance
   - title accessor returns the title string
   - frame accessor returns the Rectangle
   - buffer accessor returns a non-nil CellBuffer
   - buffer dimensions match frame dimensions
   - contentView accessor returns the provided view
   - contentController accessor returns the provided controller
   - Window new creates instance with defaults (nil contentView, nil frame, etc.)

2. RENDERING TESTS (displayOn:):
   - After draw, buffer is not nil
   - Top-left corner has char 9484 (box-drawing top-left)
   - Top-right corner has char 9488
   - Bottom-left corner has char 9492
   - Bottom-right corner has char 9496
   - Top edge (between corners) has char 9472 (horizontal line)
   - Left edge (between corners) has char 9474 (vertical line)
   - Close box [X] chars present at expected positions on title bar row:
     At (w-4, 1) = 91 '[', (w-3, 1) = 88 'X', (w-2, 1) = 93 ']'
   - Title text chars present at expected positions (x=1, y=1)
     Create window with title 'Hi', verify char at (1,1) = 72 ('H'), (2,1) = 105 ('i')

3. EVENT HANDLING TESTS:
   - handleEvent: with nil controller does not crash
   - handleEvent: delegates to contentController (create a mock controller that records calls)

4. PROTOCOL TESTS:
   - buffer returns CellBuffer (same one set during construction)
   - frame returns Rectangle (same one set during construction)

Test infrastructure:
- Create a MockController subclass of Object with instanceVars: events
  - method: initialize [ events := Array new ]
  - method: handleEvent: evt [ events := events copyWith: evt ]
  - method: events [ ^events ]
  - method: eventCount [ ^events size ]

- Use Alto::Window, Alto::Rectangle, Alto::CellBuffer with full namespace prefixes.

Runner class:
  WindowTests subclass: Object
    classMethod: run [
      | t |
      t := T new.
      ... run all tests ...
      t report.
      ^t failed
    ]

To run: mag -m 'WindowTests.run' src/display/Rectangle.mag src/display/CellBuffer.mag src/mvc/View.mag src/mvc/Controller.mag src/widgets/Window.mag test/window_test.mag

CRITICAL SYNTAX RULES:
- ALL temp vars at top of method, never mid-block
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use isNil ifTrue:
- Use copyWith: for array append (not add:)
- Follow mvc_phase3_test.mag patterns exactly
- Character codes as integers
- CellBuffer cellAt:y: returns a Dictionary; access char via (cell at: #char)
- Load order matters: source files before test files
